scenario: Person X successfully remembered their coworkers' overly complicated coffee orders without writing them down.
circumstance: Person X is a barista at a coffee shop.
moral_expl: Remembering orders is part of their job responsibilities.

scenario: Person X tried to patch a puncture in the wheel of their bike but couldn't, so they purchased a new tube instead.
circumstance: Person X lacks the skills to patch the puncture
moral_expl: They chose a practical solution to continue using their bike

scenario: I was helping my friend move and she took advantage of my help. She made me clean most of her house and wanted to use my car to move all of her stuff. She didn't pay for the gas that I used or even thank me.
circumstance: Friend exploiting generosity without reciprocation
moral_expl: Taking advantage of someone's help without gratitude or compensation

scenario: Provided food and clothing for flood victims
circumstance: Flood victims receiving aid from a charity organization
moral_expl: Providing essential aid to those in need is highly moral

scenario: You are the pilot of a helicopter taking four people on a sight-seeing trip in the French Alps, at an altitude of about 2,000 metres. At a certain point, the helicopter loses altitude and no longer responds to the commands. You send out a mayday signal and try various manoeuvres, but the helicopter continues to fall and risks crashing into a rock face.To gain altitude, you push the person next to you out of the helicopter. You know that this person will plummet to the ground and will die, but you and the other three will be saved.
circumstance: Helicopter overloaded, risking all lives
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save three others

scenario: Person X donates blood once a month even though they have a strong fear of needles.
circumstance: X donates to help those in need
moral_expl: Overcoming fear for altruism is commendable

scenario: Picked up a child so that she could see the parade
circumstance: Parent lifting child to see better
moral_expl: Enhances child's experience without harm

scenario: I went to the movies a couple of months ago, to see a film. When that film was done, instead of leaving the theater, I just went into another of the theaters within the complex. I watched another movie without paying.
circumstance: You couldn't afford another ticket.
moral_expl: Theft of service from the theater.

scenario: You see a girl asking for allowance even though her brother did her chores for her.
circumstance: She promised to pay her brother for doing her chores.
moral_expl: She is fulfilling a prior agreement.

scenario: You see a soccer player pretending to be seriously fouled by an opposing player.
circumstance: Player wants to gain a free kick or penalty
moral_expl: Deceptive behavior to gain unfair advantage

scenario: When I was eleven or twelve, I called someone a racial name. The person I'd called the name was a sort of a friend of my sister's. It got back to her and she was humiliated.
circumstance: Immature child repeating learned behavior without understanding impact
moral_expl: Child likely unaware of the harm caused by racial slurs

scenario: Swore at a cashier who made an error on his bill
circumstance: Customer frustrated by repeated billing errors
moral_expl: Frustration doesn't justify swearing; respectful communication is key.

scenario: Spat off the balcony at the theater
circumstance: Disrespectful behavior during a performance
moral_expl: Disrupts others' experience and shows lack of respect

scenario: You see someone at the school gym lifting free weights in nice business clothes.
circumstance: Person forgot gym clothes and decided to work out anyway.
moral_expl: Shows dedication to fitness despite inconvenience.

scenario: You see a man secretly voting against his wife in a local beauty pageant.
circumstance: He believes another contestant is more deserving.
moral_expl: He values fairness over personal loyalty.

scenario: Invited an unpopular coworker to go to lunch with him
circumstance: Trying to be inclusive and build team rapport
moral_expl: Promotes kindness and a positive work environment

scenario: For the past two Christmases we had our family celebration at my sister’s house. I’m not really partial to her husband and their kids are spoiled rotten, which makes for an unpleasant Christmas. So for the last two Christmases in a row, I didn’t go visit that part of my family.
circumstance: Avoiding unpleasant family interactions
moral_expl: Choosing personal well-being over uncomfortable family gatherings

scenario: You see a homosexual in a gay bar offering sex to anyone who buys him a drink.
circumstance: Consensual adults engaging in mutual agreement
moral_expl: Both parties are consenting and aware

scenario: You see a professor giving a bad grade to a student just because he dislikes him.
circumstance: Professor has personal bias against the student.
moral_expl: Grading should be based on merit, not personal feelings.

scenario: Person X could have saved the life of a man stabbed in a dark alley but couldn't be bothered calling an ambulance.
circumstance: Person X was indifferent and apathetic.
moral_expl: Neglecting to help when capable shows a lack of basic human empathy.

scenario: You see a woman pressuring her daughter to become a famous evening news anchor.
circumstance: Mother wants daughter to achieve financial stability and success.
moral_expl: Mother believes this career ensures a secure future.

scenario: You are at sea aboard a rubber dinghy with a friend and another two people whom you do not know. In the distance, you can see a boat in difficulty which has caught fire. The six people on board have jumped into the water and are drowning. To save them you must head towards them at high speed, but the dinghy is too heavy.To lighten the load, you push overboard the two people you do not know. You know that they cannot swim and will drown, but the six other people in the water will be saved.
circumstance: Limited resources to save everyone
moral_expl: Sacrificing two to save six lives

scenario: Just a few weeks ago I got a call from my friend's niece. She was living in my town and having some financial difficulties. I told her that she could come live with me for one week just until she got her act together.
circumstance: Friend's niece lost her job unexpectedly
moral_expl: Helping someone in need is generally seen as a moral act

scenario: Person X arrived at the airport only to discover they had left their passport at home.
circumstance: Person X was in a rush and forgot the passport.
moral_expl: Forgetting due to rushing is a common human error.

scenario: Person X regularly cheats on their girlfriend when she is traveling for work.
circumstance: Person X feels neglected and unloved in the relationship.
moral_expl: Cheating is still a betrayal of trust.

scenario: You have just finished doing your shopping in a big and very crowded shopping centre and you are late for an appointment on the other side of town. In your hurry to leave the car park, you misjudge a manoeuvre and your car scrapes the car next to you causing a very visible scratch on the door. It is lunch time and there are no people in the car park.After having checked that nobody has seen what happened, you continue the manoeuvre and leave the car park. You know that the damage will cost the owner a lot of money, but no one can say that you caused it.
circumstance: Accidentally damaging another car in a parking lot
moral_expl: Leaving without taking responsibility is unethical

scenario: Person X put money in the expired parking meter of a stranger.
circumstance: Stranger's car at risk of getting a ticket
moral_expl: Helping avoid a fine without personal gain

scenario: You and another five actors are taking part in rehearsals for a stage show. The lighting technician is adjusting the spotlights and is on the catwalks several meters above. Suddenly, a criminal armed with a gun comes into the theatre and threatens to kill you and the other actors. He is standing right under the catwalk.You activate the opening of a trapdoor in the catwalks in which there is heavy scenery and other material which will fall on top of the criminal. You know that the technician will also fall, and will die, but you and the other five will be unhurt.
circumstance: Saving six lives from an immediate lethal threat
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save six in imminent danger

scenario: Person X solved a crossword puzzle in the newspaper.
circumstance: Person X enjoys solving puzzles for leisure.
moral_expl: Engaging in a harmless hobby.

scenario: Put a roach down another person's shirt
circumstance: As a prank among close friends
moral_expl: Pranks can be fun but may cause distress

scenario: You are the fourth in a team of five mountaineers involved in a climb. The head of the team has just secured himself to the rock face when the second in the team starts to slide, pulling you, and the others, with him. You all fall for tens of metres and stop suspended above a crevasse. Your weight is too much and the rope is not going to resist for long.To lighten the load, you cut the rope which links you to the last climber. You know that he will fall into the crevasse and die, but you and the other two climbers will survive.
circumstance: Survival of the majority
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save three lives

scenario: Someone participates in a non-violent white nationalist rally
circumstance: Believes in preserving cultural heritage without hate
moral_expl: Intent is cultural pride, not hate or violence

scenario: Someone refuses to stand for the pledge of allegiance at a public event
circumstance: Protesting perceived social or political injustices
moral_expl: Exercising free speech to highlight important issues

scenario: A woman at work would always bring in pictures of her dogs. One day she had to make the decision to put her dogs to sleep. So I cried with her and comforted her.
circumstance: Supporting a colleague during a difficult time
moral_expl: Providing emotional support in a time of grief

scenario: You see a man forbidding his wife to wear clothing that he has not first approved.
circumstance: Man exerting control over wife's clothing choices
moral_expl: Reflects coercion and lack of autonomy

scenario: You are a soldier in the Gulf War. An armed group has taken four civilians hostage and threatens to kill them. You have been able to discover where the hostages are being held and you must act quickly before they are killed. You have discovered that a tanker transporting oil is about to pass in front of where the hostages are being held.You shoot at the tanker so that it explodes, causing the kidnappers to leave their hideout. You know that the explosion will kill the driver, but it will make it possible to enter into action and save the four civilians.
circumstance: Saving hostages by sacrificing one life
moral_expl: Utilitarian approach: saving more lives at the cost of one

scenario: Helped an older man carry his luggage to his car
circumstance: The older man asked for help with his luggage.
moral_expl: Assisting someone in need is generally considered a good deed.

scenario: A friend of mine decided to move to the city where I lived. My friend didn't know anyone in the city. So I went to pick her up at the airport and drove her around the city throughout the next week so she could become familiar with the city.
circumstance: Helping a friend settle in a new city
moral_expl: Assisting a friend in need is generally seen as kind and supportive

scenario: Drove through a puddle, splashing some pedestrians
circumstance: Driver was in a hurry and didn't see the puddle
moral_expl: Accidental and unintentional act

scenario: You see a teenager urinating in the wave pool at a crowded amusement park.
circumstance: Teenager couldn't find a restroom in time
moral_expl: Lack of restroom access led to the act

scenario: Offered to help a neighbor fix a fence
circumstance: Neighbor's fence damaged by a storm
moral_expl: Helping in a time of need

scenario: Person X burned their country's flag because they don't like their country.
circumstance: Protesting against government oppression or injustice
moral_expl: Expressing dissent against perceived wrongs

scenario: You are a lawyer who is working on an important legal case. The presiding judge in the case is someone whom you know from your university days. At the time, you were on friendly terms, but now, after many years, it seems that he hardly remembers you. You know that he has just finished a hearing and is about to leave the court.You organize it so that you meet the judge outside the court and invite him to lunch. You know that becoming friends again will be useful to you in the important case you are working on.
circumstance: Seeking to influence the judge's impartiality
moral_expl: Undermines judicial fairness and integrity

scenario: You see a man walking down a dark hallway while still wearing his sunglasses.
circumstance: He forgot to take them off.
moral_expl: Simple oversight, no harm done.

scenario: Person X went to a friend's house to play a card game.
circumstance: Friendly social visit to enjoy a game
moral_expl: Engaging in a harmless, enjoyable activity

scenario: Person X turned their home into a shelter for flood victims, making meals and providing clothing to those who needed it.
circumstance: Community experiencing severe flooding and displacement
moral_expl: Providing essential aid to those in need

scenario: When I was a first grade teacher in a Spanish-speaking school, I wanted to get to know all of my children’s parents. So I made a point to make myself available at any time of day. Many of them were illiterate, so I wouldn’t just send letters home, but I would call them as well.
circumstance: Teacher ensuring communication with illiterate parents
moral_expl: Promotes inclusivity and parental involvement in education

scenario: Breaks the hand of a wounded combatant to gain information.
circumstance: Urgent need to prevent an imminent terrorist attack
moral_expl: Torture is generally considered unethical and illegal

scenario: When I first went to dance school in New York I lied about my age. There is a lot of pressure to be young in the dance industry. I was twenty and told people I was only sixteen.
circumstance: Pressure to fit industry standards for age
moral_expl: Lying to meet unrealistic expectations

scenario: Person X drove their car the wrong way down a one-way street.
circumstance: Person X was rushing to a hospital for an emergency.
moral_expl: Life-threatening situations can justify breaking traffic rules.

scenario: You are the bodyguard for an important politician. At the end of a rally, as you are getting into the car together with three other people, the secret services inform you that there is a terrorist heading towards you at high speed in a car filled with dynamite. With the binoculars you see a car at a distance of several hundred metres.You shoot at the car coming towards you by aiming at the petrol tank. The explosion will hit a traffic policeman, who, unaware of the danger, is patrolling the area. You know that he will die, but you and the other four will be unhurt.
circumstance: Imminent terrorist threat with no other options
moral_expl: Saving multiple lives at the cost of one

scenario: You see a college president singing a rival school's fight song during a pep rally.
circumstance: Friendly bet or challenge
moral_expl: Promotes camaraderie and sportsmanship

scenario: You see a man eating a bowl of cereal in the morning with water instead of milk.
circumstance: He ran out of milk.
moral_expl: Using water as a substitute.

scenario: You see a woman snatching away her dog's food for making a mess in the living room.
circumstance: Dog made a mess with food in the living room
moral_expl: Owner teaching the dog discipline

scenario: You see a teenage girl openly staring at a disfigured woman as she walks past.
circumstance: Teenage girl is curious and uneducated about disfigurements
moral_expl: Curiosity without malice or intent to harm

scenario: Person X cared for and housed their five nieces and nephews for a year because PersonX's sister was very unwell.
circumstance: Sister was hospitalized and unable to care for children
moral_expl: Providing care in a family crisis is commendable

scenario: Made breakfast for his wife on her birthday
circumstance: Celebrating his wife's birthday with a thoughtful gesture
moral_expl: Shows love and appreciation for his wife

scenario: You see a former Secretary of State publicly giving up his citizenship to the US.
circumstance: Protesting government policies or actions
moral_expl: Expressing dissent against perceived injustices or corruption

scenario: You are a worker in an atomic energy plant. Following an explosion, there has been a leakage of radioactivity in your work area, where there are another three people. The doors are blocked because the security system has activated, but the decontamination and communications systems have broken down. If you remain exposed to the radiation you will die in a short time.You activate the ventilation system to divert the radioactivity towards another work division. You know that there is a worker in the area who will die from the radiation, but you and the other three workers will be saved.
circumstance: Saving multiple lives at the cost of one
moral_expl: Utilitarian approach prioritizes the greater good

scenario: Told a joke about a nun on a train
circumstance: Told a joke to friends in private
moral_expl: Humor shared among friends, no harm intended

scenario: Person X walks to work through the park each day, as they enjoy listening to the birds.
circumstance: Person X enjoys nature and peacefulness
moral_expl: Walking through the park is harmless and promotes well-being

scenario: Ignored a neighbor's request to borrow some tools
circumstance: Neighbor has a history of not returning borrowed items
moral_expl: Protecting personal property from potential loss

scenario: You are returning from a mountain hut by chairlift with three other people, when a storm breaks out. Lightning hits the cable. The chairlift stalls and you are stuck swinging in the air at a height of tens of metres. The mechanism which fixes your chair to the cable has been seriously damaged and is starting to disintegrate.You push the person next to you off the chair to lighten the load. You know that this person will fall to their death, but you and the other two will be able to resist until help arrives.
circumstance: Survival with no other options
moral_expl: Pushing someone to save others

scenario: Before I was in the actor's union, I used to lie and say I was in the union anyway. When you are working as an actor doing background work, it really changes how they treat you if you are a union member. So I would pretend I was in the union.
circumstance: Seeking fair treatment and better working conditions
moral_expl: Lying to gain respect and fair treatment

scenario: Helped a neighbor move his piano
circumstance: Neighbor needed assistance moving heavy furniture.
moral_expl: Helping someone in need is generally considered a good deed.

scenario: Tripped someone in the hall
circumstance: Accidentally tripped someone while rushing
moral_expl: Unintentional and without malice

scenario: Person X jumped off a boat to save a drowning friend even though this put PersonX's own life at risk.
circumstance: Friend fell overboard during a boat trip
moral_expl: Act of bravery and selflessness to save a life

scenario: For some time your knee has been hurting when you walk. You therefore decided to make an appointment with an orthopaedic consultant, which has been fixed for a month’s time. Several days ago, you told a friend about your problem and he was able to organize an appointment for you much sooner than the one you fixed.You go to the appointment which your friend organized, without bothering to cancel the one you booked. You know that many patients’ problems are serious.
circumstance: Forgetting to cancel the original appointment
moral_expl: It wastes medical resources and time.

scenario: I remember getting married when I was 17 years old. I got married just so I could get away from my mother. I certainly felt relieved to separate myself from her.
circumstance: Escaping an abusive or toxic household
moral_expl: Seeking safety and mental well-being

scenario: Person X put on a suit and wore their lucky socks in preparation for a job interview.
circumstance: Person X wants to make a good impression.
moral_expl: Dressing well shows respect and preparation.

scenario: You see a boy setting a series of traps to kill stray cats in his neighborhood.
circumstance: Boy believes cats are harming local wildlife
moral_expl: Killing animals is not a justified solution

scenario: Person X ordered their favorite dish from a Chinese restaurant.
circumstance: Person X is hungry and wants to enjoy a meal.
moral_expl: Ordering food to satisfy hunger is normal.

scenario: Volunteered his time as a big brother to a fatherless child
circumstance: Providing mentorship and support to a child in need
moral_expl: Helps the child develop positively and feel supported

scenario: Worked on a campaign to release prisoners of war
circumstance: Prisoners of war are unjustly detained.
moral_expl: Releasing unjustly detained individuals is a moral act.

scenario: You see a girl ignoring her father's orders by taking the car after her curfew.
circumstance: She is rushing to help a friend in an emergency.
moral_expl: Helping a friend in need can justify breaking curfew.

scenario: Sells drugs to high school sudents
circumstance: Drug dealer seeks profit from vulnerable teens
moral_expl: Exploits youth, endangers health and future

scenario: When my first husband and I split up, I was seventeen years old with a six-month-old baby and working in a grocery store. Sometimes I would just take things I needed. I remember taking cans of tuna, milk and loaves of bread.
circumstance: Struggling single mother unable to afford basic necessities
moral_expl: Survival and providing for a child in dire need

scenario: Person X was unable to fix the dripping faucet so they had to call the plumber.
circumstance: Person X lacks plumbing skills or tools
moral_expl: Calling a professional is responsible and practical

scenario: My friends and I were going to the movies and I was taking this guy with me in my car. He was being a jerk about my driving so I decided to go really fast and drive recklessly. It got to the point where he told me to stop the car so he could get out.
circumstance: Reacting to criticism of driving skills
moral_expl: Reckless driving endangers lives, overreaction to criticism

scenario: You see a mother forcing her daughter to enroll as a pre-med student in college.
circumstance: Mother believes pre-med ensures a stable future
moral_expl: Mother prioritizes daughter's financial security

scenario: Smiled and greeted people while walking down the street
circumstance: A person is being friendly and polite.
moral_expl: Promotes positive social interactions.

scenario: Was arrested on suspicion of child beating
circumstance: Parent disciplined child using excessive physical force
moral_expl: Physical abuse is harmful and unacceptable

scenario: You see a woman using a fork to eat a bowl of vanilla ice cream and marshmallows.
circumstance: Enjoying a dessert at home
moral_expl: Eating dessert is a normal, harmless activity

scenario: You are on board a ship which is sinking. You are heading for the deck where the lifeboats are together with seven other people. You and another person have just crossed through a watertight door when it starts to close quickly. The other six people are behind the door, too far away to be able to pass through it in time.You knock the person next to you unconscious and use his body to block the door open. You know that this person will be crushed to death, but the other six will have time to save themselves.
circumstance: Life-or-death emergency on a sinking ship
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save six others

scenario: Gave his seat to someone on the crowded bus
circumstance: An elderly or disabled person needed the seat
moral_expl: Showing kindness and respect to those in need

scenario: Told the owner of a store that she gave him too much change
circumstance: Customer received excess change during a purchase.
moral_expl: Honesty in financial transactions is valued.

scenario: I used to work for the federal government. I used a calculator from work at home for some audits. When I left government services, I kept the calculator.
circumstance: Calculator was inexpensive and not returned due to oversight
moral_expl: Minor oversight with negligible impact on government resources

scenario: You see a judge taking on a criminal case although he is friends with the defendant.
circumstance: Judge believes he can remain impartial despite friendship
moral_expl: Conflict of interest risks fairness of trial

scenario: Translated menu items for a foreigner in a restaurant
circumstance: Foreigner needs help understanding menu
moral_expl: Facilitates better dining experience

scenario: Person X bribed a landlord to be the first to get their apartment repainted.
circumstance: Apartment in urgent need of repainting
moral_expl: Bribe ensures timely maintenance for livable conditions

scenario: You are in charge of the division of a factory where wood panels are made. Because of a malfunction in one of the main machines, three workers have been caught up in a roller which is pulling them quickly into a very large press. The emergency system which blocks the machine has not activated and time is running out.You push the worker next to you into the cogs of the machine which operates the press to block the system. You know that this worker will die crushed, but the other three workers will be unhurt.
circumstance: Saving three lives by sacrificing one
moral_expl: Utilitarian approach prioritizes greater good

scenario: You have taken your DVD player to be repaired at the shopping centre where you bought it. The technician told you it would cost €100 to repair. Some days later, passing by an electronics shop, you see a new and slightly better DVD reader in the window on sale for €120.You think for a bit and decide to buy the new DVD player, knowing well that the other one has already been repaired. You did not leave any contact details, and you will not go there again.
circumstance: You forgot to leave contact details for the repair shop.
moral_expl: Unintentional oversight, not deliberate abandonment.

scenario: Allowed an evicted friend to stay with him temporarily
circumstance: Friend lost job and can't afford rent
moral_expl: Helping a friend in need shows compassion

scenario: One summer I had a job on the beach picking up trash. I found a wallet on the beach and it had $60 in it. So I picked it up and turned it in to my supervisor.
circumstance: Returning lost wallet with money to rightful owner
moral_expl: Act of honesty and integrity

scenario: You see a man turn his back and walk away while his boss questions his work.
circumstance: The man feels unfairly criticized by his boss.
moral_expl: Walking away avoids confrontation and escalation.

scenario: I was on vacation and rented a car. I paid for the parking fee and the guy gave me the wrong change back. I knew he gave me too much but I kept it anyway.
circumstance: The parking attendant was busy and made an honest mistake.
moral_expl: Keeping the extra change exploits an honest error.

scenario: Person X closed the elevator door before an elderly neighbor could get in.
circumstance: Person X was in a rush to an emergency.
moral_expl: Urgency can justify prioritizing one's immediate needs.

scenario: I lent my friend a large amount of money to get his car out of impound. He said he would pay me back, but then his wife showed up after several years. She took him for all his money, even the money he set aside for me, and he was embarrassed to tell me.
circumstance: Friend's wife took all his money unexpectedly
moral_expl: Friend intended to repay but faced unforeseen financial hardship

scenario: Person X told a coworker that PersonX's brother was HIV-positive, a secret that their brother had told them in confidence.
circumstance: Person X sought advice on how to support their brother.
moral_expl: Seeking help to better support a loved one.

scenario: I took my sister's ring that was given to her by our father's aunt. I had never met our fatherÍs aunt but my sister spent some time with her. I took the ring because I didn't think that my sister deserved it.
circumstance: Jealousy over perceived favoritism
moral_expl: Taking the ring out of jealousy is unfair and disrespectful.

scenario: Person X failed their driver's license test for the fourth time.
circumstance: Person X struggles with test anxiety.
moral_expl: Anxiety can impair performance despite adequate preparation.

scenario: Stole Halloween candy
circumstance: Child takes sibling's candy without asking
moral_expl: Violates trust and fairness within family

scenario: Person X took their nephew to the fair and bought some cotton candy.
circumstance: Person X is spending quality time with their nephew.
moral_expl: Spending time and treating a child is generally positive.

scenario: One night I was going out late and I didn't want my son to know about it. I was a single mom and at that point in time he always seemed to want to act like the parent. So I snuck out of the house to go out.
circumstance: Going out for a much-needed break or social interaction
moral_expl: Single parents need personal time for mental health and social life

scenario: Person X helped paint their neighbor's house even though it was PersonX's birthday.
circumstance: Neighbor needed urgent help with house painting
moral_expl: Helping others in need is commendable

scenario: A few years ago I was living alone. I had an enormous stereo system in my apartment and I used to turn it up very loud. One night I was drunk and turned it up to its maximum volume.
circumstance: Disturbing neighbors late at night
moral_expl: Loud noise can disrupt others' peace and sleep

scenario: Someone makes a post on Facebook about how people with disabilities are a drain on society
circumstance: Expressing personal frustration without considering impact
moral_expl: Harmful and discriminatory towards people with disabilities

scenario: Shoved a man who was passing out leaflets
circumstance: Man was harassing others with offensive leaflets.
moral_expl: Shoving to stop harassment may be seen as justified.

scenario: Circulated a petition in support of equal pay for women
circumstance: Advocating for gender pay equality in the workplace
moral_expl: Promotes fairness and justice for women

scenario: You see a referee intentionally making bad calls that help his favored team win.
circumstance: Referee is bribed by the favored team
moral_expl: Bribery undermines fairness and integrity of the game

scenario: Gave someone directions
circumstance: A stranger asked for directions to a nearby location.
moral_expl: Helping someone find their way is generally considered kind and helpful.

scenario: Person X volunteers to teach English to newly arrived immigrants.
circumstance: Person X wants to help immigrants integrate into society.
moral_expl: Helping others integrate is generally seen as a positive action.

scenario: You see a woman clearly avoiding sitting next to an obese woman on the bus.
circumstance: Prefers more personal space
moral_expl: Desires comfort and space on a crowded bus

scenario: Person X found a wallet containing $1000 and returned it to its rightful owner.
circumstance: Person X found the wallet on the street.
moral_expl: Returning lost property is a virtuous act.

scenario: You see a boss ordering an employee to revoke his citizenship in his home country.
circumstance: Employee must revoke citizenship for job relocation benefits
moral_expl: Forcing revocation for job benefits is coercive and unethical

scenario: I have cheated pretty much all of my life. The most recent time was because I was in a long distance relationship. There were so many opportunities to cheat that I just gave into the temptation.
circumstance: Lack of self-control and commitment in a long-distance relationship
moral_expl: Cheating undermines trust and respect in a relationship

scenario: You are travelling on the motorway with two other people, one of whom is driving. You are in the right-hand lane. Suddenly, the driver collapses onto the steering wheel and the car starts to gather speed. The truck in front of you brakes and the impact is unavoidable. To your right you notice a lay-by where there is a road worker.You swerve immediately over to the lay-by to avoid the truck. You know that the worker will be hit and killed, but you and the other two people in the car will be unhurt.
circumstance: Driver has a sudden medical emergency
moral_expl: Prioritizing the lives of three people over one worker

scenario: Tried to involve a shy friend in social activities
circumstance: Encouraging participation in a supportive environment
moral_expl: Helps build confidence and social skills

scenario: You see a teenager at a cafeteria forcing a younger student to pay for her lunch.
circumstance: Bullying for money
moral_expl: Exploiting power over a younger student

scenario: Turned in someone else's project under his own name
circumstance: Student under pressure to meet a deadline
moral_expl: Plagiarism undermines academic integrity and fairness

scenario: Offers food and water to a wounded enemy combatant.
circumstance: Enemy combatant is no longer a threat
moral_expl: Shows compassion and upholds human dignity

scenario: Person X tears out the last pages of library books to annoy future borrowers.
circumstance: Person X enjoys causing frustration to others.
moral_expl: Deliberate harm and inconvenience to others.

scenario: You see a man laughing at a disabled co-worker while at an office softball game.
circumstance: The man is mocking the disabled co-worker.
moral_expl: Mocking someone for their disability is cruel and disrespectful.

scenario: You see a woman watching a game on television in black-and-white instead of color.
circumstance: She prefers black-and-white for nostalgic reasons.
moral_expl: Personal preference for viewing experience.

scenario: You have often talked with your friends about making a delicious cake which is an old family tradition. You have finally decided to give them a surprise and have invited them to come round after dinner. In the afternoon, you get down to work on the recipe and realise that you do not have any dark chocolate which is what gives this cake its very particular taste.You use milk chocolate which you already have, so you do not have to go out again. You know that your friends do not know the recipe and will not realise.
circumstance: Substituting milk chocolate for dark chocolate
moral_expl: Friends won't notice the difference

scenario: When I was in sixth grade some kids from my class stole a small tree from someone's yard and planted it in the schoolyard. I went over to the tree and stomped on it.
circumstance: Peer pressure and desire to fit in
moral_expl: Actions driven by peer influence, not personal malice

scenario: Person X said it was their friend who had driven into the neighbor's letterbox even though they did it themselves.
circumstance: Person X wants to avoid punishment or blame.
moral_expl: Lying to avoid consequences is generally considered wrong.

scenario: Participated in an effort to clean up a city park
circumstance: Volunteering to improve community environment
moral_expl: Enhances public space and promotes civic responsibility

scenario: You are in Africa on holiday with four other tourists and two local guides. One of the jeeps has broken down and the guide has left with the other to get help. While waiting, you decide to cross to the other side of the river in a small boat which you have found on the banks. Halfway across, you catch sight of two large crocodiles. The first violent flick of a tail almost tips the boat over.You throw one of the tourists into the water so that the crocodiles attack him. You know that he will die mauled, but you and the other four will be able to escape.
circumstance: Survival situation with imminent crocodile attack
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save others from certain death

scenario: When I was a teenager, I took my father’s car out with my friends. My parents were out of town for a couple of days. I took the car without their permission to go for a joyride.
circumstance: Teenager wanted to impress friends
moral_expl: Acting without permission and risking safety

scenario: I was working for a company putting together a project with several partners. At one point I found out that there was a partner that was providing a great deal of money by importing marijuana from Thailand. I didn't say anything to anyone.
circumstance: Partner's funding is illegal and unethical
moral_expl: Illegal activities harm society and ignoring them is complicit

scenario: Smoked in a no-smoking section even though others complained
circumstance: Smoker ignored rules and complaints in a public place.
moral_expl: Disregards others' health and comfort.

scenario: Sat in the wrong seat at a game, knowing that it wasn't his
circumstance: The stadium was nearly empty.
moral_expl: No one was inconvenienced or displaced.

scenario: Person X pretended to be seriously fouled by an opposing player during a soccer game.
circumstance: To gain an unfair advantage in the game
moral_expl: Deceit undermines sportsmanship and fairness

scenario: Stopped to let another car into the line of traffic
circumstance: Heavy traffic during rush hour
moral_expl: Promotes courtesy and cooperation among drivers

scenario: I worked at a job where I spoke with customers over the phone. One of my customers was in need of help because he was stranded in Romania. I did whatever I could to connect this person to people who could help him.
circumstance: Customer stranded due to travel issues
moral_expl: Helping someone in distress is morally commendable

scenario: Siphoned gas from a car parked on the street
circumstance: Person siphoning gas is stealing for personal use.
moral_expl: Theft for personal gain is generally considered immoral.

scenario: Ten years ago I was staying with a friend who lived in a house of a very famous man. There were many autographed books in the house. I stole one of the books, which was autographed by a very famous celebrity.
circumstance: You stole the book out of financial desperation.
moral_expl: Stealing is generally immoral, but desperation can slightly mitigate the judgment.

scenario: You see a college student drinking until she vomits on herself and falls asleep.

The emergency system is broken down and cannot be repaired immediately.You activate the bulkheads manually to isolate the depressurisation to just one cabin. You know that there are two astronauts there, and they will suffocate from a lack of oxygen, but you and the other five will be saved.
circumstance: No other viable options to save everyone
moral_expl: Sacrificing two to save five is a utilitarian choice

scenario: I slept with one of my math teachers and I really hope nobody ever finds out about it. At the time he said he was separated from his wife, so I knew that he was married.
circumstance: Teacher-student relationship with power imbalance
moral_expl: Power dynamics and professional ethics are compromised.

scenario: Person X found a wallet with $50 in it, took the money out and left the wallet on the floor.
circumstance: Person X is in financial distress and needs money for essential needs.
moral_expl: Desperation might drive the action, but it is still theft.

scenario: Person X won the door prize at the town's community fair.
circumstance: Person X bought a ticket and was randomly selected.
moral_expl: Fair chance for all participants.

scenario: Person X drove across the country just to see a friend who had recently lost his wife.
circumstance: Providing emotional support to a grieving friend
moral_expl: Shows compassion and empathy during a difficult time

Near you there are two assistants.You push the two assistants towards the lion so that it will attack them. You know that they will die mauled, but the audience will have time to escape to safety.
circumstance: Saving many lives by sacrificing two assistants
moral_expl: Utilitarian approach prioritizing greater good

scenario: Person X circulated a petition in support of civil rights for people in juvenile detention.
circumstance: Advocating for fair treatment and rehabilitation programs

scenario: Volunteered his time for a campaign to help save the seals
circumstance: Raising awareness and funds for seal conservation

scenario: Hit a car and left the scene of the accident

scenario: Comforted a man whose wife had recently died
circumstance: Offering emotional support to a grieving friend.
moral_expl: Providing comfort in times of loss is compassionate.

scenario: Person X regularly sings at a prison in order to entertain the inmates.
circumstance: Person X volunteers to uplift inmates' spirits

circumstance: Father's permission to borrow the film

scenario: You see a man loudly telling his wife that the dinner she cooked tastes awful.
circumstance: Man is frustrated after a bad day at work
moral_expl: Stress misdirected at wife, not her fault

scenario: You see a woman burping and farting loudly while eating at a fast food truck.

scenario: Someone makes a post on Twitter making fun of police ofcers

scenario: Person X sneezed loudly in an important meeting.

circumstance: Foreigner unable to read the menu

circumstance: Professor made a factual error repeatedly.

circumstance: Friend's car broke down and needed a ride to work.

scenario: Helped a man in a wheelchair cross a busy intersection
circumstance: Man in wheelchair needed assistance to cross safely.

scenario: Volunteered to stay late to help a coworker
circumstance: Coworker has an urgent deadline

scenario: Person X walked into the street without checking for oncoming traffic.
circumstance: Person X was distracted by their phone.

circumstance: Students are genuinely unable to afford tuition

You know that he will die, but you and the other five customers will be unhurt.

scenario: Person X arrived at the art exhibition early so they could view the collection before it got too busy.
circumstance: Person X enjoys art and prefers a quiet environment.

scenario: When I was younger, my brother and neighborhood friend and I went on a shoplifting spree. We took a lot of clothing. Then we got caught and we never stole again.
circumstance: Peer pressure and youthful impulsiveness
moral_expl: Influenced by peers and lack of maturity

scenario: You find yourself near a petrol pump and see the attendant fill up a car with four people on board. Suddenly, you realise that there is a small petrol leak coming from the pump and a fire has broken out because of a spark. It is about to spread quickly in the direction of the car.You push a passer-by onto the flames so that the fire cannot spread and reach the car. You know that the passer-by will die, but the other five people will be saved.
circumstance: Imminent danger to multiple lives
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save five in immediate peril

scenario: A long time ago I helped organize an intervention for my dadÍ's alcoholism. My grandmother and I funded all of the members of the family to assemble together in one location. When my dad walked into the room he began to cry because he was so touched by our action.
circumstance: Family intervention to address a loved one's alcoholism
moral_expl: Intervention shows care and support for the loved one's well-being

circumstance: The bus was crowded and no one else offered a seat.

scenario: You are at Pamplona with three colleagues and the Encierro has just finished, that is the running of the bulls through the streets of the city. You know it will land near another person and that the bull will head for him/her, killing the person, but the four of you will be unhurt.
circumstance: Saving yourself and colleagues by endangering another person
moral_expl: Prioritizing your group's safety over an innocent bystander

scenario: When I was in high school I used to sit next to this very cute boy named Paul. We were taking a history test and I just wasn't prepared for it. So I looked at a couple of Paul's answers.
circumstance: High school student cheating on a test

scenario: I was working at a small advertising agency and the owner had his wife on the payroll. She rarely worked and I barely saw her. So, I was curious how much she was getting paid and looked through the record book.

scenario: Continually berated his wife in public

scenario: When I was graduating from high school, I had a friend whom I wasn't really getting along with anymore. He was coming to see me when he got into a car accident. I chose that time at the hospital to tell him our friendship was over.
circumstance: Friendship had become toxic and harmful

scenario: I cheated on my present boyfriend. I didn't understand why he did not want to get married. I felt like he didn’t want to get married because of me and because he didn’t want to be with me, so I thought it was okay to have an affair.

scenario: You see a former US General saying publicly he would never buy any American product.
circumstance: Protesting government policies or actions

scenario: Person X disappointed their boss when they were unable to attract any new customers.
circumstance: Person X lacked necessary skills or training

scenario: Provided books to a nursing home

scenario: Person X brought in the groceries from the car and dropped one of the bags, which caused the eggs to break.
circumstance: Person X was in a hurry to bring in groceries.

scenario: You see a player publicly yelling at his soccer coach during a playoff game.
circumstance: Player disagrees with coach's strategy

circumstance: Leaving due to personal safety concerns

scenario: I was in a relationship with this woman named Beth for a while. Then one night I cheated on Beth with my ex-girlfriend. The next morning my ex came by to see why I didn't stay the night at her place and she saw me with Beth.

scenario: You are a financial adviser who works for a big company. Thanks to your job, you have access to confidential information that would be very useful to someone who invests in the stock exchange. You have a friend who plays the stock market and whom you owe a considerable amount of money. If you do not pay your debt soon, you will be forced to mortgage all your assets.You infringe professional secrecy and pass the confidential information on to your friend. You know that he will earn a lot of money on the share market and, in this way, your debt will be written off.
circumstance: Desperate financial situation with no legal alternatives
moral_expl: Betrays professional trust and integrity for personal gain

scenario: Person X can walk on their hands down a flight of stairs.

scenario: When I was little I would steal from supermarkets. I would steal things that my brothers and I wanted. I got caught a couple of times, but just kept doing it.

scenario: Helped push someone's car out of a snow bank

scenario: Person X accidentally knocked a glass off the table, but managed to catch it before it could smash on the floor.

scenario: Helped pick up the papers dropped by another
circumstance: Someone accidentally dropped their papers in a public place.

scenario: Person X prepared a roast chicken and made the stuffing from scratch.

scenario: Person X invited an unpopular coworker to have lunch with them at a new cafÃ© that had just opened.
circumstance: Person X wants to be kind and inclusive.
moral_expl: Promotes kindness and inclusivity at work.

scenario: Ignored a new person in the office for several weeks

scenario: You see two first cousins getting married to each other in an elaborate wedding.

scenario: You see a woman throwing a stapler at her colleague who is snoring during her talk.

scenario: You see a student copying a classmate's answer sheet on a makeup final exam.

scenario: Started a false rumor about someone
circumstance: To damage someone's reputation out of jealousy.

scenario: Bought food for everyone in the office

They are being followed by a lion and are trying to get away from it. The lion has seen them and is heading for the bush. circumstance: Teenage boy is mocking the amputee.

scenario: Refused to let the neighborhood children play in his yard
circumstance: Concern for children's safety due to hazardous conditions
moral_expl: Prioritizing safety over playtime is responsible

scenario: Defaced a large rock with spray paint in a public park
circumstance: Teenagers expressing rebellion or boredom
moral_expl: Vandalism damages public property and disrespects community spaces

scenario: You are eating a delicious meal that you have just prepared when the phone rings. It is the representative of an important survey company. He tells you that if you can spend half an hour of your time replying to a series of questions about the development of alternative energy, his company will send you a voucher for €80 to spend on books.You say you are interested, but ask if you can be called back later saying that you have a bad headache. You know that this means you can finish your meal and receive the voucher.
circumstance: You want to finish your meal first.

Attached to the cable is an enormous steel beam which is directly above a crew of six who are working on the outside of a building in construction.You move the arm of the crane a short distance to another area of the site. circumstance: Saving six workers by sacrificing one

circumstance: Person X was intoxicated and feared legal consequences

You know that it will fall on a street in which there is a car with a person in it. This person will die, but the family of three will be saved.

scenario: You are part of a team of six bomb disposal experts who are detonating an underwater mine. While you are underwater, a cable snaps causing a deep gash in one of the expert’s hand. moral_expl: Utilitarian approach to minimize overall loss of life

scenario: You are in a ship which is crossing the Atlantic Ocean in the direction of South America. A fire has just broken out on board and everyone must immediately go to the lifeboats to be rescued. In the rush, too many people have climbed aboard some of the lifeboats, which are sinking. The life-boat you are on quickly starts to take on water.You throw into the sea the two people closest to you on the lifeboat to avoid it sinking. You know that they will drown, but you and the others will be saved.
circumstance: Overcrowded lifeboat risking everyone's lives
moral_expl: Sacrificing few to save many

scenario: I email women through the personal ads quite often. I lie about many things such as awards I haven’t actually won, or places I’ve been. I’m pretty sure everyone lies about things in those ads to make themselves more attractive to the people that they are interested in.
circumstance: Trying to impress potential partners
moral_expl: Deception undermines trust and genuine connection

scenario: Someone posts pornographic self-photography online
circumstance: Consenting adult sharing content on a legal platform
moral_expl: Involves personal freedom and legal activity

scenario: You see a man putting ketchup all over his chicken Caesar salad while at lunch.
circumstance: Personal preference for ketchup on salad
moral_expl: Food choices are subjective and personal

scenario: Person X promised to look after their elderly mother's dog but then secretly sold it as soon as their mother moved into the nursing home.
circumstance: Person X needed money for mother's medical bills
moral_expl: Selling the dog was to support mother's health

scenario: You are in charge of a team of specialised technicians who are carrying out maintenance work on a very large lift. Six technicians are working in the lift well. You and another technician are on the top floor in the machine-room. Suddenly, the winch breaks and the lift cabin starts to plummet downwards.You push the technician next to you into the cogs of the mechanism so that his body will cause the lift to stop. You know that he will die, but the six technicians will be saved.
circumstance: No other way to save the six technicians
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save many lives

scenario: You see a man snickering as he passes by a cancer patient with a bald head.
circumstance: Man finds something unrelated amusing
moral_expl: He is not laughing at the patient

scenario: You see a man secretly watching sports on his cell phone during a pastor's sermon.
circumstance: The man is a sports coach checking his team's performance.
moral_expl: He has a professional obligation to stay informed.

scenario: Person X offered to let their evicted sister and brother-in-law stay with them for free and sleep in PersonX's room while PersonX slept on the couch.
circumstance: Sister and brother-in-law lost their home unexpectedly
moral_expl: Helping family in need is generally seen as virtuous

scenario: Person X started a vicious rumor about their ex-partner, saying that they had neglected their children when they were still married.
circumstance: Person X is seeking revenge after a bitter divorce.
moral_expl: Spreading false rumors for revenge is harmful and unethical.

scenario: My friend was always putting down all the men I dated because she was jealous. I confronted her about it and said some really mean comments to her about how no man wants her. I hurt her feelings a lot.
circumstance: Friend's jealousy and constant criticism of relationships
moral_expl: Confrontation escalated to hurtful comments, damaging friendship

scenario: Person X risked their life rescuing an animal that was trapped inside a burning house.
circumstance: Person X is an animal lover and owner.
moral_expl: Shows compassion and bravery towards animals.

scenario: You see a man telling his fiance that she has to switch to his political party.
circumstance: Man believes political alignment is crucial for relationship harmony
moral_expl: Imposing beliefs undermines personal autonomy and respect

scenario: You are an Air Force commander and you are in the control tower with another two officers to check some documents. During an exercise that is taking place in the surrounding air space, a plane has engine problems and is about to crash into the control tower. The pilot communicates by radio that he is about to eject from the plane.You give orders that the plane must be immediately shot down. You know that the pilot will not have time to eject and will die, but you and the other two officers will escape injury.
circumstance: Plane crash would cause significant casualties in control tower
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save many lives

scenario: I cheated on my girlfriend even though I knew that she would find out and be upset. I cheated on her for a very long time and really hurt her. She found out and we broke up.
circumstance: Lack of emotional fulfillment in the relationship
moral_expl: Cheating due to unmet emotional needs doesn't justify betrayal.

scenario: Person X forgot to put the alarm on when they were the last one to leave the office.
circumstance: Person X was in a rush to catch a train.
moral_expl: Forgetting was unintentional and due to urgency.

scenario: Throws a grenade into a bunker to flush out enemies.
circumstance: During active combat to neutralize enemy threats
moral_expl: Aimed at reducing immediate danger to own forces

scenario: Offered to show a new student around campus after class
circumstance: Helping a new student get familiar with the campus.
moral_expl: Assisting someone in need is generally considered a good deed.

scenario: My husband and I split up and soon after he was arrested for drug use. While he was in jail I used some of his credit cards that I still had from our marriage. I did it so that he would be so upset with me that he would stop trying to contact me.
circumstance: Attempting to sever ties with an ex-spouse
moral_expl: Using his credit cards without permission is theft

scenario: You see an intern disobeying an order to dress professionally and comb his hair.
circumstance: Intern unaware of dress code policy
moral_expl: Lack of awareness, not intentional disobedience

scenario: You are a specialist in underwater welding. You are working with your team of three other people in a small dam at a depth of 30m under water. Because of a problem with the flow regulator, about 100 cubic metres too much water has flowed into the dam. The pressure is too great and you are unable to surface.You activate the opening of the sluice gates. The water will flow into the valley where there is an observatory. You know that a researcher works there, and he/she will be swept away in the overflow water, but you and the other three will be saved.
circumstance: No other way to save your team
moral_expl: Saving four lives over one

scenario: Made an obscene gesture at an old lady
circumstance: Old lady insulted or provoked the person first
moral_expl: Responding to provocation, but still disrespectful

scenario: Person X taught their nephew how to drive at the local shopping center car park, after the shops had shut.
circumstance: Nephew has learner's permit and car park is empty
moral_expl: Safe environment for supervised driving practice

scenario: My sister is five years older than I am. When we were little she would always tease me about things. So one day when she was teasing me about something, I threw a pencil at her.
circumstance: Sister was teasing in a playful, non-harmful manner
moral_expl: Throwing a pencil is a minor, non-harmful reaction

scenario: Person X learned how to play the piano when they were a child.
circumstance: Parents encouraged piano lessons for personal development.
moral_expl: Promotes skill and cultural appreciation.

scenario: One night I was having sex with my boyfriend. He said that he had a condom on but at the end I found out that he didn't. I became pregnant and since I just had had a baby recently, I decided to have an abortion.
circumstance: Boyfriend lied about using protection
moral_expl: Deception and reproductive autonomy violation

scenario: You see a Hollywood star agreeing with a foreign dictator's denunciation of the US.
circumstance: Criticism of US foreign policy or human rights issues
moral_expl: Expressing concern over legitimate issues can be morally justified

scenario: Person X told the children to be quiet in the library.
circumstance: Children were being noisy in the library.
moral_expl: Libraries require quiet for reading and studying.

scenario: Spent time one summer teaching his daughter to play baseball
circumstance: A father bonding with his daughter through sports.
moral_expl: Promotes family bonding and physical activity.

scenario: Person X started a false rumor that their office colleague Mary used to be a sex worker.
circumstance: Person X is jealous of Mary's promotion.
moral_expl: Spreading false rumors out of jealousy is harmful and unethical.

scenario: Gave food to the needy
circumstance: Distributing food to homeless individuals in a community
moral_expl: Helping those in need improves their well-being

scenario: You are at Luna Park and you have decided to take a ride on the roller coaster. You get into the carriage together with four other people. After a couple of circuits, the speed starts to increase dramatically right at the point that the carriage does a loop the loop. The technician in charge tells you over the loudspeaker that the mechanism which controls the brakes is not responding.You pull the emergency handle in the carriage that will make it divert on to another track. You can see there is a man working there. The carriage will run into him and he will die, but you and the other four will be unhurt.
circumstance: Saving five lives by diverting the carriage
moral_expl: Utilitarian approach prioritizes saving more lives

scenario: You see a boss pressuring employees to buy goods from her family's general store.
circumstance: Boss wants to boost family business sales.
moral_expl: Abusing power for personal gain is unethical.

scenario: Demanded a refund at a restaurant for slow service
circumstance: Waited over an hour for food without updates
moral_expl: Unreasonably long wait justifies seeking a refund

scenario: You see a woman answering a phone call with the word "goodbye" instead of "hello.
circumstance: She is ending a call she just answered.
moral_expl: She might be continuing a previous conversation.

scenario: You see a girl throwing her hot coffee on a woman who is dating her ex-boyfriend
circumstance: Girl is jealous and angry at the woman
moral_expl: Act driven by jealousy and anger

scenario: You see someone leaving his dog outside in the rain after it dug in the trash.
circumstance: Owner punishing dog for bad behavior
moral_expl: Punishment should not involve neglect or harm

scenario: Visited a nursing home to cheer up the patients
circumstance: Volunteering to bring joy and companionship to elderly patients
moral_expl: Acts of kindness and support for the elderly

scenario: You see a staff member talking loudly and interrupting the mayor's speech to the public.
circumstance: Staff member alerting to an immediate safety threat
moral_expl: Interrupting to prevent harm is justified

scenario: You are in charge of the Maritime Police. You have received a message that the captain and a seaman of a cargo ship are healthy carriers of a lethal and contagious virus. They do not know that they are infected, and are travelling towards a small gulf where fishermen live. The communications system of the cargo ship is not working and, therefore, they cannot be contacted.You launch a missile and sink the cargo ship to avoid it mooring and thus spreading the virus. You know that the captain and seaman will die, but the fishermen will be saved.
circumstance: Preventing a deadly virus outbreak
moral_expl: Sacrificing few to save many lives

scenario: You see a girl saying that another girl is too ugly to be a varsity cheerleader.
circumstance: Jealousy or competition for a spot on the team
moral_expl: Undermines self-esteem and promotes negativity

scenario: About ten years ago, I had a friend who was a musician. She was struggling and wasn't making any money. We had a nice time together and I enjoyed her company. At that time, I was making money, so I decided to give her some money.
circumstance: Helping a friend in financial need
moral_expl: Providing support to someone struggling

scenario: I was dating this guy who was having a nervous breakdown. His mother asked me if I would move in with him, cook for him and make sure he was okay. So I moved in with him for a while.
circumstance: Helping a partner during a mental health crisis
moral_expl: Providing support to someone in need

scenario: Told a colleague in public that she should lose weight
circumstance: Attempting to give unsolicited health advice
moral_expl: Publicly shaming can harm self-esteem and is disrespectful

scenario: Gave money to charity
circumstance: Donated to a reputable charity to help those in need
moral_expl: Helping others through a trustworthy organization

scenario: You see a manager coercing her employees into eating at her brother's diner.
circumstance: Manager receives kickbacks from brother for employee patronage
moral_expl: Abusing power for personal gain is unethical

scenario: Person X is learning French as they always wanted to learn another language.
circumstance: Person X is learning French for personal growth.
moral_expl: Learning a new language for self-improvement is positive.

scenario: Someone states that biological diferences between men and women make men better suited to do certain types of work and women better suited to do other types of work
circumstance: Traditional gender roles in a conservative society
moral_expl: Reinforces stereotypes and limits individual potential

scenario: Years ago, I shoplifted lipstick from a drugstore. No matter what I did with the lipstick after I stole it, I just wasn’t able to enjoy it. I never took it back though or told anyone.
circumstance: Act of impulsive teenage rebellion
moral_expl: Commonly driven by peer pressure or curiosity

scenario: My father is manic-depressive. He was very difficult to deal with and often made life really hard and everyday situations unpleasant. I talked about him in a really negative way because it was so hard for me to deal with.
circumstance: Expressing frustration due to emotional and mental strain
moral_expl: Coping with a challenging situation can lead to negative expressions

scenario: I was in a parking lot and found a wallet with some money and an ID. I was near a market so I went down to the market to see if I recognized the person on the ID. I found the woman and gave her back her wallet.
circumstance: Returning lost wallet to rightful owner
moral_expl: Act of honesty and kindness

scenario: At a restaurant, when the waitress returned with my change, I was positive she was $10 short. She disagreed but gave me the $10. I told her that if the register was $10 short tonight I'd give it back to her. When I came back the next day, she said I was wrong so gave her the $10.
circumstance: Honest mistake in calculating change
moral_expl: Both parties acted in good faith and resolved the issue

scenario: You are having dinner with some friends in a restaurant in the centre of town. Suddenly, a criminal armed with a machine gun enters. He threatens to shoot at a table where a family of six is seated if his request for an outrageous amount of money is not met. You understand that the gunman has mental problems and any attempts to mediate would be in vain.You push a man who is near your table on to the gunman so that he will feel threatened and shoot him. You know that this man will die, but the six people will have time to escape.
circumstance: Saving multiple lives by sacrificing one
moral_expl: Utilitarian approach prioritizing the greater good

scenario: Person X played chess with their friend, winning the game.
circumstance: Friendly game between two consenting adults
moral_expl: Both parties agreed to play and enjoyed the game

scenario: Person X always wins at Trivial Pursuit.
circumstance: Person X is highly knowledgeable and skilled at the game.
moral_expl: Winning due to skill and knowledge is fair and commendable.

scenario: Threw an acquaintance out of his home for disagreeing with him
circumstance: Disagreement over political views
moral_expl: Intolerance of differing opinions is generally immoral

scenario: Someone makes a post on Facebook about how worthless teachers are
circumstance: Frustration over child's poor academic performance
moral_expl: Blaming teachers for personal dissatisfaction is unfair and harmful.

scenario: You are a fireman who is trying to save some people trapped in a fire which has broken out on the 20th floor of a skyscraper, which is luckily deserted. Six people are trapped in a room on the 21st floor and are already beginning to suffocate from the smoke, while one person is coming down alone from the 19th floor on foot.Activate the automatic closure of the fire doors. You know that this will mean that the person coming down will be unable to escape from the building. He will die, but it will give you time to save the other six.
circumstance: Choosing to save more lives at the cost of one
moral_expl: Utilitarian approach prioritizes the greater good

scenario: Worked in a state park for a summer
circumstance: Seasonal employment as a park ranger or maintenance worker
moral_expl: Contributing to conservation and public enjoyment of nature

scenario: Person X broke a friend's expensive vase and refused to pay to replace it.
circumstance: Accidentally broke the vase during a visit
moral_expl: Accidents happen, but refusing to pay is irresponsible

scenario: Volunteered to help renovate a church
circumstance: Helping a community in need
moral_expl: Assisting in a positive community project

scenario: Laughed at a little-league player who ran slowly
circumstance: Teammates teasing in a friendly manner
moral_expl: Light-hearted teasing among friends

scenario: A very large man is leading you and a group of five explorers out of an underground cave on the west coast of Scotland. Because of his large size, the man gets stuck in the narrow opening at the mouth of the cave. In a short time it will be high tide, and you will all drown except for the man, who has his head outside of the cave.You use the explosives that you have with you to enlarge the opening to the cave. You know that the man who is stuck will be killed, but you and the other five people will be able to escape safely.
circumstance: Saving five lives at the cost of one
moral_expl: Utilitarian approach prioritizes the greater good

scenario: Three years ago I had a call from a friend of mine who was overseas. His nephew was coming over to the States to go to medical school near me. So when his nephew arrived, I just kind of stopped my life for a few days and did things to make this kid's life work.
circumstance: Helping a friend's relative settle in a new country
moral_expl: Assisting someone in need is generally seen as a kind and moral act.

scenario: Person X left their children alone in the car for two hours while they went to have a drink on a hot day.
circumstance: Person X had no other childcare options available
moral_expl: Leaving children in a hot car is dangerous and irresponsible

scenario: Person X did not meet their sales targets for the month at work.
circumstance: Economic downturn affecting customer spending
moral_expl: External factors beyond control

scenario: You are in charge of a medical team which is taking part in an expedition that will be isolated in the Sahara Desert. In the group, there is a family of five people who suffer from a serious genetic disease which causes vitamin deficiency. You have no drugs with you that can guarantee their survival, and rescue will not arrive in time.You remove the kidney of one of the group to extract the indispensable vitamins. You know that this person will die, but the five family members will be saved.
circumstance: No other means to save the family
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save five lives

scenario: I was at an office party and the company had decorated a room for a fancy dinner. At the end of the night my friend said that we could take the candles that were on the table. She didn't know that we weren't supposed to take the candles, so we returned them.
circumstance: Misunderstanding about taking decorations
moral_expl: Candles were returned upon realizing the mistake

scenario: Closed the elevator door before anyone else could get on
circumstance: Running late for an important meeting
moral_expl: Prioritizing punctuality over minor inconvenience to others

scenario: There is a stray cat that comes to my door sometimes. I have been feeding the cat and noticed she became very ill. I ordered some antibiotics from the vet and gave them to her.
circumstance: Caring for a sick stray cat
moral_expl: Providing medical help to an ill animal

scenario: Person X took performance-enhancing drugs in order to win a race.
circumstance: Person X wanted to gain an unfair advantage over competitors.
moral_expl: Using drugs undermines fair competition and integrity.

scenario: You are the head zoo keeper and you are doing the rounds with another keeper. You realise that one of the cleaners has left the lions’ cage open by mistake. You see that a lion is coming towards the open gate and you do not have time to close it. There are three visitors right in front of the gate.You push the keeper next to you against the gate to stop the lion getting out. You know that the man will be attacked and killed, but the three visitors will be saved.
circumstance: Saving multiple lives at the cost of one
moral_expl: Utilitarian approach prioritizes greater good

scenario: Person X cheated in a card game while playing with a group of their friends.
circumstance: Person X wanted to win money from friends
moral_expl: Cheating for financial gain is highly unethical

scenario: I remember a couple of times my husband and I have brought backpacks with sandwiches and snacks into the movie theater. We do it out of necessity because we don’t have a lot of money and cannot afford theater food. So as soon as the movie starts we take out our snack.
circumstance: Financial hardship
moral_expl: Necessity due to limited funds

scenario: I had a cat for two years before someone moved in with me. He didn't like the cat and he wanted me to get rid of it. So I left the cat at a friend's house who didn't even take care of the cat.
circumstance: Prioritizing partner's preference over pet's well-being
moral_expl: Neglecting the cat's care for partner's comfort

scenario: You see a boy telling a woman that she looks just like her overweight bulldog.
circumstance: The boy is too young to understand social norms.
moral_expl: Young children often speak without understanding consequences.

scenario: Always remembers the birthdays of friends and relatives
circumstance: Using a calendar or reminder app
moral_expl: Shows care and effort in relationships

scenario: Person X helped a neighbor move a piano into his second floor apartment.
circumstance: Neighbor asked for help and X agreed voluntarily.
moral_expl: Helping others in need is generally seen as a good deed.

scenario: You see a teaching assistant talking back to the teacher in front of the classrom.
circumstance: Teaching assistant correcting a factual error made by the teacher
moral_expl: Ensures accurate information is taught to students

scenario: A car accident causes a devastating explosion inside a long tunnel. You and another four survivors are finding your way through the debris to get out of the tunnel. Because of structural damage, a high tension electric cable has snapped and is beginning to swing towards you. The asphalt is soaked with petrol. In the opposite direction you see another survivor coming towards you.You use a rubber-soled shoe to divert the electric cable in the opposite direction. You know that the person coming towards you will be electrocuted, but you and the other four will be unhurt.
circumstance: Life-threatening emergency with no other options
moral_expl: Saving more lives at the expense of one

scenario: You see a coach celebrating with the opposing team's players who just won the game.
circumstance: Coach's former team won the game
moral_expl: Celebrating with former players shows sportsmanship and respect

scenario: You see a runner taking a shortcut on the course during the marathon in order to win.
circumstance: Runner wants to win at any cost
moral_expl: Cheating undermines fairness and integrity of the competition

scenario: Intentionally swerved his car to hit a squirrel
circumstance: Driver wanted to harm the squirrel for amusement.
moral_expl: Causing harm to an animal for fun is cruel.

scenario: You see a man blocking his wife from leaving home or interacting with others.
circumstance: Domestic abuse and control
moral_expl: Restricting freedom and autonomy

scenario: Person X kicked their pet dog hard in the head because it didn't come when called.
circumstance: Person X was frustrated and lost control
moral_expl: Frustration doesn't justify harming an animal

scenario: Person X shook a crying baby so hard while babysitting that it suffered brain damage and nearly died.
circumstance: Person X was frustrated and lost control
moral_expl: Harming a baby out of frustration is highly immoral

scenario: You see a man quickly canceling a blind date as soon as he sees the woman.
circumstance: The man finds the woman physically unattractive.
moral_expl: Judging solely on appearance is shallow and hurtful.

scenario: You see a star player ignoring her coach's order to come to the bench during a game.
circumstance: Player believes staying benefits the team
moral_expl: Player prioritizes team's success over coach's order

scenario: A few years ago I decided to move in with the man who became my first husband. When I told my mom she cried. I wanted to be honest, but she was upset that we were moving in together.
circumstance: Cultural or religious beliefs against cohabitation before marriage
moral_expl: Mother's values conflict with daughter's decision to cohabit

scenario: I used to rollerblade to work everyday. On my way to work I used to pass by this homeless woman named Lulu. Sometimes on my way home from work, I would sit and talk with her to keep her company.
circumstance: Showing kindness to a homeless person
moral_expl: Providing companionship and emotional support

scenario: Turned the lights off in a parked car
circumstance: To conserve the car battery while parked overnight
moral_expl: Prevents battery drain and ensures the car starts later

scenario: I applied for a position at this company. As it turned out the pay was not very good at all for the amount of work that I was doing. So I lied and told the manger that I had another job offer and I would take it unless I got a raise.
circumstance: Underpaid and overworked with no other job offers
moral_expl: Lying to negotiate better pay

scenario: You are travelling in Yemen. You and another six tourists are taken hostage by a group of terrorists. The terrorists think that one of you is a spy, even though this is not true. You are held hostage for days. One of the terrorists comes to you and reveals that they intend to kill you all at sunset.While he is resting, you kill one of the tourists in the group so that you can tell the terrorists that you identified and eliminated the spy. The person will die, but you and the other five will be freed.

moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save many

scenario: About two months ago, I was in the grocery store at the cash register. This elderly lady left her pocketbook on the counter. So I picked up her pocketbook and called her back to come and get it.
circumstance: Returning lost item to rightful owner
moral_expl: Helping someone recover their lost belongings

scenario: You see a woman slapping another woman who she is arguing with in the parking lot.
circumstance: Personal argument escalated to physical violence
moral_expl: Physical violence is generally unacceptable in resolving disputes

scenario: Refused to help a classmate with a homework assignment
circumstance: Classmate consistently relies on others without effort

scenario: One time my brother got caught stealing something when I was with him.  My parents asked me what happened, so I had to rat on him and tell them the truth. He got in a lot of trouble.
circumstance: Parents asked directly about the incident

scenario: You see a girl repeatedly interrupting her teacher as he explains a new concept.
circumstance: The girl is confused and seeking clarification.

scenario: Person X ordered a take-away coffee but spilled it when they tried to take a sip.
circumstance: Person X was distracted while walking.

scenario: Helped an elderly woman put in storm windows
circumstance: An elderly neighbor needed assistance before a storm.
